name: Proto-Malayo-Polynesian (family node)
notes: STRUCTURAL ANCESTRY NODE (not a modelled G2P target). Proto-Malayo-Polynesian is the reconstructed ancestor of the Malayo-Polynesian branch of Austronesian, covering Malay/Indonesian, Javanese, Tagalog and the wider Pacific. It exists here only so the ancestry / phylogenetic-distance metrics of the modern varieties that reference it have a node to resolve to; NO phonology is claimed or reconstructed and the minimal grapheme set is a placeholder (afa.json / trk.json stub pattern).

sources.author: Blust, R.
sources.title: The Austronesian Languages
sources.notes: Reference for Austronesian branching (structural node only).

location.notes: Glottolog's computed centroid for the Malayo-Polynesian family — a summary of a whole clade's range, not a place anyone speaks.
